events.title: Hundreds feared dead in Lebanon strikes
events.summary: The United Nations has strongly condemned airstrikes by the Israeli military across Lebanon on Wednesday which have resulted in significant casualties and destruction.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 8 April: US-Iran ceasefire announced; strikes continue in Lebanon
events.summary: After nearly 40 days of intense hostilities across the Middle East – marked by rising civilian casualties and widespread damage to critical infrastructure – a two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran has been announced. While...

events.title: World News in Brief: Death on the Mediterranean, displacement in Afghanistan, ‘One Health’ summit
events.summary: Over 180 people are feared dead or missing in the latest shipwrecks on the Mediterranean, according to the UN migration agency, IOM.

events.title: Security Council: Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: The UN Security Council has failed to adopt a resolution aiming to boost security in the Strait of Hormuz as the critical shipping corridor remains largely closed to global trade and the transport of humanitarian aid, with war continuing t...

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 7 April: Humanitarian and economic pressures grow; Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: Hostilities continue across the Middle East, with ongoing strikes, rising civilian impacts and mounting pressure on critical infrastructure and humanitarian services. On the diplomatic track in New York, China and Russia vetoed a Security...

events.title: ‘Entire families brutally erased’: Remembering the victims of the 1994 genocide against the Tutsi in Rwanda
events.summary: Thirty-two years ago, a genocidal campaign was unleashed against Rwanda’s Tutsi minority, resulting in more than one million deaths. On Tuesday, the UN is holding commemorations to ensure that the genocide is never forgotten and never repe...

events.title: World News in Brief: ‘Skyrocketing’ needs outpace Sudan funding, Ukraine strikes update, global water security
events.summary: The UN is significantly scaling up its presence in the Sudanese capital, Khartoum, to expand life-saving operations as the conflict between rival militaries approaches its third year.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 6 April: Strikes persist across region as humanitarian needs rise
events.summary: Strikes and counter-strikes continue across the Middle East, with dozens of casualties reported over the weekend in Lebanon following Israeli strikes targeting the south and the capital, Beirut. Meanwhile, humanitarian needs are rising, cr...

events.title: UN nuclear agency chief ‘deeply concerned’ by reports of latest attack on Iran power plant
events.summary: Reports of yet another projectile strike near the Bushehr nuclear power plant prompted Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), to register his deep concern on Saturday.

events.title: ‘We watched them die before our eyes’: Sudan health workers helpless amid medical shortages
events.summary: As violence forces tens of thousands to flee Sudan’s South Kordofan state, doctors in a key maternity hospital are facing impossible choices – with too few supplies, too many patients, and lives slipping away.

events.title: Deminers race to keep up with military technology
events.summary: In conflict zones where new technologies are making landmines more dangerous, deminers must innovate at the same pace to avoid being left behind, a leading UN mines expert has told UN News.

events.title: World News in Brief: South Sudan rights, opioid guidelines update, DR Congo crisis continues
events.summary: South Sudan is evolving into a catastrophic human rights and humanitarian crisis, UN Human Rights Council-appointed independent experts warned on Thursday.

events.title: Guterres warns of ‘wider war’ as Middle East conflict enters second month
events.summary: The Middle East crisis has lurched into its second month, prompting UN Secretary-General António Guterres to issue a stark warning on Thursday morning that the world is “on the edge of a wider war” with catastrophic global implications.

events.title: Middle East crisis exposes global energy fault line as UN urges shift to renewables
events.summary: The ongoing crisis in the Middle East is exposing a central vulnerability in the global economy: the dependence on fossil fuels flowing through regions affected by conflict, a situation which is strengthening the UN’s case for a faster tra...

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 2 April: Civilian impact deepens as UN chief says ‘we must find a peaceful way out’ before region is engulfed by war
events.summary: Deadly new strikes reported across the Middle East overnight and Thursday rattled energy markets and pushed crude oil prices up to $107 in early trading. Hopes dwindled of a quick end to the conflict as President Trump spoke of another "tw...
